chunks.page_summary: This page details the Modbus RTU RS-485 data types used by Rheonics devices, including floating point and integer types, and a comprehensive breakdown of parameter status bits indicating various error, warning, and operational conditions.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
Data-Types (Modbus RTU RS-485)
Modified on: Fri, 3 Jul, 2020 at 2:03 PM

*   Float: IEEE754 floating point. This data type spans two registers which should be read together. Depending on the endianness of the host it might be necessary to swap high/low byte and/or the respective registers.
*   Int16: Signed 16 bit integer (register)
*   Uint16: Unsigned 16 bit integer
*   Parameter status: The parameter status is a bitmask of individual status bits. As a general rule Bit 0 checking should be implemented where a set bit corresponds to an error/warning condition. In case of a critical error the parameter value is also set to NAN (not a number – See IEEE754)
    *   Bit 0: General error – Check other bits for more detailed configuration
    *   Bit 1: Internal configuration error – Restart. If error persists call Rheonics for support
    *   Bit 2: Hardware error, e.g. broken temperature sensor. Call Rheonics for support and arrange RMA
    *   Bit 3: Dependency error: a parameter is calculated from other parameters and one of the source parameter has an error. Check all other parameters for errors.
    *   Bit 4: Device is starting up and no results are ready.
    *   Bit 5: Internal error – Restart. If error persists call Rheonics for support.
    *   Bit 8: Parameter result is not stable. Check measurement conditions (flow, vibration, etc.)
    *   Bit 15: Parameter exceeds sensor upper limit. No results available.
    *   Bit 14: Parameter below sensor lower limit. No results available.
    *   Bit 13: Parameter value above upper warning range. Measurements might not be accurate and sensor performance might be reduced.
    *   Bit 12: Parameter value below lower warning range. Measurements might not be accurate and sensor performance might be reduced.
key_facts: Rheonics devices use Modbus RTU RS-485 for communication.
The Float data type is an IEEE754 floating point, occupying two registers.
Int16 is a signed 16-bit integer, and Uint16 is an unsigned 16-bit integer.
The Parameter status is a bitmask, where Bit 0 indicates a general error or warning.
Bit 1 signifies an internal configuration error.
Bit 2 indicates a hardware error, such as a broken temperature sensor.
Bit 3 indicates a dependency error where a calculated parameter's source parameter has an error.
Bit 4 indicates the device is in a startup phase, and results are not yet available.
Bit 8 indicates that the parameter result is not stable.
Bit 15 signals that the parameter has exceeded the sensor's upper limit.
Bit 14 signals that the parameter is below the sensor's lower limit.
Bit 13 indicates the parameter value is above the upper warning range.
Bit 12 indicates the parameter value is below the lower warning range.
warnings_notes: For the Float data type, it might be necessary to swap high/low bytes or registers depending on the host's endianness.
As a general rule, Bit 0 of the parameter status should be checked to identify error/warning conditions.
If a critical error occurs, the parameter value will be set to NAN (Not a Number) as per IEEE754.
In case of Bit 1 or Bit 5 internal errors, a restart is recommended; if the error persists, Rheonics support should be contacted.
A Bit 2 hardware error, such as a broken temperature sensor, requires contacting Rheonics for support and to arrange an RMA.
When a Bit 3 dependency error occurs, all other parameters should be checked for errors.
For a Bit 8 unstable parameter result, measurement conditions (like flow, vibration) should be checked.
When Bit 15 or Bit 14 are set (parameter exceeds or is below sensor limits), no results will be available.
If Bit 13 or Bit 12 are set (parameter in warning range), measurements might not be accurate, and sensor performance might be reduced.
